narrative.headline: 2018 Nissan Maxima: brakes lead a small set of owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 36 complaints with NHTSA, a relatively low total. Service brakes top the list at 22%, followed by forward collision avoidance (17%) and the electrical system (14%). No deaths are reported. One brake complaint mentions a fire, and the structure category, though small, is tied to the only crash and only injury in the data, so both areas are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 8 reports (22%), including 1 with fire. Check the brakes carefully and confirm any open recall has been completed.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 6 reports (17%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. Owners describe the collision-avoidance system behaving unexpectedly.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 5 reports (14%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. Complaints point to sensor and warning behavior during normal driving.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Only 2 reports (6%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. A small but recurring complaint area.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: Just 2 reports (6%) but the only entries tied to harm in this data: 1 crash and 1 injury. Small in count, worth attention by outcome.
